evidence_summary: A three-day montage documenting the technical construction of a large green silk balloon. The sequence must visually communicate the progression from raw materials to a functional flight apparatus through increasing scale and assembly complexity.

A three-day period where Dorothy and Oz work together to construct a large green silk balloon. They apply glue to make it airtight, attach a basket, and prepare a heating mechanism using wood provided by the Tin Woodman.
From heavy labor to focused anticipation.
Raw green silk and glue containers on the ground.
A completed balloon with an attached basket and heating mechanism ready for flight.
Dorothy and Oz assemble a functional hot air balloon using silk, glue, and wood.
